manus.name: Manus (Meta)
manus.summary: Acquired by Meta for $2B in December 2025 and embedded directly into Ads Manager as of February 17, 2026. Handles report building, audience research, competitor analysis, and budget optimization — all without leaving your ad account.
manus.best_for: Meta advertisers who want automated reporting, audience insights, and competitive intel without a separate tool
manus.limitations: Meta-only — useless if you don't run Facebook/Instagram ads. Still rolling out gradually (some accounts see it as 'Business AI Beta'). Can hallucinate campaign metrics — always sanity-check numbers.
manus.cost: Included with Meta Ads Manager (no extra charge)
manus.starter_prompt: Show me a performance breakdown of my top 5 ad sets this month — cost per result, ROAS, and spend. Flag any that are underperforming compared to last month and tell me what you'd change.
getting_started_steps: Open Meta Ads Manager at adsmanager.facebook.com
Look for the 'Tools' menu in the top navigation — Manus AI appears there (or as 'Business AI Beta' under Engage Audience in Business Suite)
If you don't see it yet, check back in a few days — the rollout is gradual
Once visible, click it, paste the starter prompt below, and review the output
Ask follow-up questions in the same chat window — it has context of your account
suggested_prompts: Analyze my ad spend this month and flag underperforming campaigns compared to last month
What audience segments should I test next based on my best-performing ad sets?
Build me a weekly performance report I can share with my team — include ROAS, CTR, and spend by campaign
What are my competitors in [your industry] doing with their Meta ads right now?

claude_cowork.name: Claude Cowork
claude_cowork.summary: Launched January 2026, Cowork turns Claude into a desktop agent that autonomously reads, creates, and edits files on your computer. Give it a folder and a goal — it works through tasks without constant hand-holding. Think of it as leaving instructions for a highly capable coworker who runs while you're in meetings.
claude_cowork.best_for: Knowledge workers who need to process, summarize, or transform large amounts of documents — contracts, reports, research, meeting notes
claude_cowork.limitations: Requires Claude Pro ($20/mo) or Max ($100-200/mo) — no free tier. No persistent memory between sessions. Still labeled 'research preview' as of March 2026. Available on Mac and Windows desktop only (not web or mobile).
claude_cowork.starter_prompt: Go through the files in this folder and create a summary document listing each file's purpose, key findings, and any action items mentioned. Save the output as a single markdown file called 'summary.md'.
getting_started_steps: Download Claude Desktop from claude.ai/download
Sign in with your Anthropic account — you'll need Claude Pro ($20/mo) or Max ($100/mo) for Cowork access
Open the app and look for the 'Cowork' tab or the Cowork option in a new chat
Select a folder on your computer to give Claude access to (it only touches what you grant)
Paste the starter prompt below — describe your task in plain English and let it run
suggested_prompts: Summarize all the documents in this folder into a one-page brief with key themes and open questions
Extract every action item from these meeting notes and organize them by owner and due date
Compare these two contracts side by side and highlight the key differences in liability, payment terms, and exit clauses
Reformat all of these reports into a consistent template and save each as a new file

openclaw.name: OpenClaw
openclaw.summary: Open-source 24/7 agent that runs in the cloud or on your own machine and talks to you via WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, or iMessage. Persistent memory that builds over weeks. Model-agnostic — use Claude, GPT, Gemini, or local models. Has 230,000+ GitHub stars. Coral (coral.inc) offers a hosted version starting at $50/mo that removes the technical setup entirely.
openclaw.best_for: Executives who want a proactive, always-on assistant that connects their entire digital life — email, calendar, Slack, CRM, and more
openclaw.limitations: Self-hosted version requires Docker/VPS setup and comfort with config files. Community skills carry real security risk (prompt injection). Token costs can balloon if not managed — easy to spend $100+ unintentionally. Coral-hosted version solves the setup problem but adds a subscription.
openclaw.cost: Free software (self-host) + VPS $5–20/mo + API costs $10–150/mo. Or Coral hosted: $50/mo (Standard) or $200/mo (Ultra)
openclaw.starter_prompt: Every morning at 8am, check my calendar for today's meetings, look up any prep materials or context about the attendees, and send me a briefing via Slack with the agenda and key talking points for each meeting.
getting_started_steps: The easiest path: sign up at coral.inc for a hosted instance (7-day free trial, $50/mo after) — no technical setup required
Connect your messaging app (Telegram, WhatsApp, Slack, or Discord) through the Coral dashboard
Connect your tools — Gmail, Google Calendar, Notion, Slack — via the Integrations tab
Send it a message in your chosen messenger describing what you want it to do
To self-host instead: visit github.com/openclaw/openclaw and follow the Docker setup guide
suggested_prompts: Every morning at 8am, send me a daily briefing via Slack: today's meetings, any prep needed, and emails that need a response
Monitor my inbox and flag anything that needs a reply within 24 hours — text me a summary each evening
Every Friday at 4pm, send me a weekly summary of my Slack channels and any open threads I should close out
When I message you a person's name, pull their last 3 emails and calendar interactions and give me a quick brief

notion_custom_agents.name: Notion Custom Agents
notion_custom_agents.summary: Launched February 24, 2026, Notion Custom Agents are proactive AI teammates that run autonomously — triggered by schedules, Slack messages, emails, or database changes. Unlike your personal Notion AI (which waits for you to ask), custom agents act on their own. They span Notion, Slack, Gmail, Calendar, Linear, Figma, and custom MCP tools.
notion_custom_agents.best_for: Teams already living in Notion who want recurring workflows automated — status reports, task routing, Q&A bots, and meeting follow-ups
notion_custom_agents.limitations: Requires Notion Business plan ($16/user/mo). Notion Credits launch May 4, 2026 ($10 per 1,000 credits) — agents are free until then. Still requires a mature Notion setup to deliver real value. Degrades with poorly structured databases.
notion_custom_agents.cost: Included in Notion Business plan ($16/user/mo) through May 4, 2026; then usage-based Notion Credits at $10/1,000 credits
notion_custom_agents.starter_prompt: Every Monday morning, scan my project tracker database for tasks that are overdue or have no update in 7+ days. For each one, draft a Slack message to the assignee asking for a status update, and add a 'Needs Follow-Up' tag to the task.
getting_started_steps: Make sure you're on Notion Business plan — visit notion.com/product/agents (link above) for details
In your Notion workspace, go to Settings → Agents and click 'New Agent'
Give your agent a name and set its trigger — a schedule (e.g., every Monday 9am), a database change, or a Slack message
Connect the tools it needs access to: your databases, Gmail, Slack, Calendar
Write its instructions in plain English — paste the starter prompt below as a starting point, then share the agent with your team
suggested_prompts: Every Monday, scan my project tracker for overdue tasks and draft Slack follow-ups to each assignee
When a new item is added to our Inbox database, triage it: assign an owner, set a priority, and post a summary in #ops-updates on Slack
After every team meeting ends on my calendar, pull the meeting notes from Notion and email a summary with action items to all attendees
Answer questions in our #hr-questions Slack channel using our Notion employee handbook — never answer outside of what's in the handbook

claude_code.name: Claude Code
claude_code.summary: Anthropic's terminal-native coding agent. Best-in-class for complex multi-file refactors, architecture analysis, and writing tests. Lives in your terminal or IDE. Not a non-technical tool — but if you have a developer on your team, this is the tool they should be using.
claude_code.best_for: Developers doing serious engineering work — multi-file refactors, security audits, test coverage, legacy code modernization
claude_code.limitations: Developer-only — requires terminal comfort. No persistent memory between sessions. Heavy usage hits rate limits on the $20 Pro plan fast; serious users need Max at $100–200/mo.
claude_code.cost: $20/mo (Pro — light use) or $100/mo (Max 5x) or $200/mo (Max 20x — all-day coding)
claude_code.starter_prompt: Explore this codebase and give me a plain-English architecture overview — what the main components are, how they connect, and where the biggest technical risks or quality issues are. Then tell me the top 3 things you'd fix first.
getting_started_steps: Subscribe to Claude Pro ($20/mo) or Max ($100/mo) at claude.ai — Claude Code requires at least Pro
Install Claude Code in your terminal: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
Navigate to your project folder in the terminal and run: claude
Paste the starter prompt below — Claude Code will explore your codebase and respond
Ask follow-up questions or give it tasks: 'fix the 3 issues you identified' or 'write tests for src/auth.js'
suggested_prompts: Walk me through this codebase in plain English — what it does, how it's structured, and where the biggest risks are
Write comprehensive tests for all functions in the src/ directory that currently have no test coverage
Find and explain every security vulnerability in this codebase, starting with the highest severity
Refactor this entire module to be cleaner and more maintainable — explain every change you make

codex.name: Codex (OpenAI)
codex.summary: OpenAI's cloud-first coding agent. Included in all paid ChatGPT plans (Plus, Pro, Business). Strong on long-horizon planning and parallel multi-file tasks. Unlike Claude Code, it runs entirely in the cloud — connect your GitHub repo and it creates PRs directly.
codex.best_for: Developers in the OpenAI ecosystem who want to delegate large coding tasks and review a finished PR rather than babysit every step
codex.limitations: Cloud-only — all code runs on OpenAI's servers. Local CLI has more latency than Claude Code. Usage is capped by plan and resets periodically — heavy users may need extra credits. Developer-only tool.
codex.cost: Included with ChatGPT Plus ($20/mo), Pro ($200/mo), Business ($30/user/mo), or API (pay per token)
codex.starter_prompt: Analyze this repository and create a detailed plan to add a REST API layer — include routing, authentication middleware, error handling, and a test suite. Show me the plan before writing any code.
getting_started_steps: Subscribe to ChatGPT Plus, Pro, or Business at chat.openai.com — Codex is included in all paid plans
Go to chatgpt.com/codex (link above) and connect your GitHub repository
Describe your task in plain English — Codex will plan and then execute it
When it's done, it will open a pull request on GitHub for you to review
Review and merge the PR — or ask for changes by commenting in the Codex chat
suggested_prompts: Add comprehensive test coverage to this project — target 80%+ for all core modules
Migrate this codebase from JavaScript to TypeScript with full type annotations
Set up a complete CI/CD pipeline for this project using GitHub Actions
Audit this codebase for security vulnerabilities and open a PR with fixes for any critical or high issues

perplexity_computer.name: Perplexity Computer
perplexity_computer.summary: Launched February 26, 2026, Perplexity Computer is a multi-model agent that coordinates 19 AI models (Claude, Gemini, Grok, and others) to complete complex, long-running workflows in the background. Give it a high-level objective — market research, competitive analysis, report writing — and it delegates each subtask to the best model for the job.
perplexity_computer.best_for: Executives and ops leaders who need deep, multi-source research done automatically — competitive intelligence, market landscapes, due diligence
perplexity_computer.limitations: Requires Perplexity Max at $200/mo. Long-running tasks can take 10–30+ minutes. Best for research and analysis, not for taking action inside other apps. Still new — reliability improving week over week.
perplexity_computer.starter_prompt: Research the top 8 competitors in [your industry]. For each one, compile: pricing model, core product features, recent product launches or announcements, estimated market position, and any publicly known weaknesses. Output as a structured comparison table.
getting_started_steps: Sign up for Perplexity Max at perplexity.ai — $200/mo, Computer access is included
Go to perplexity.ai and look for the Computer option in the sidebar or main menu
Start a new Computer session and type your research objective in plain English
Replace [your industry] in the starter prompt below with your actual industry, then paste it in
Let it run — deep research tasks take 10–30 minutes; you'll get a notification when it's done
suggested_prompts: Research our top 8 competitors in [industry] and build a full comparison matrix: pricing, features, positioning, and weaknesses
Compile every funding announcement and major product launch in [industry] from the last 60 days into a market intelligence report
Write a due diligence summary on [company name] — business model, team, traction, risks, and competitive position
Monitor these 5 company websites and send me a summary of any pricing or product changes once a week

cofounder.name: Cofounder
cofounder.summary: No-code automation platform where you describe workflows in plain English and it builds them for you. Connects 80+ tools (Gmail, Slack, Calendar, CRM, GitHub, and more). Persistent memory that learns your business context over time. Priced at $39.99/mo with 4,000 credits included — you pay per automated action, not per seat.
cofounder.best_for: Founders and ops leaders who want to automate multi-tool workflows without writing code and without hiring a developer
cofounder.limitations: Credit-based — heavy automation can burn through your monthly allotment fast ($4 per 100 credits after the included 4,000). Smaller community and skill library than n8n. Better for single-user or small-team use than enterprise.
cofounder.cost: Free trial (3,000 credits included), then $39.99/mo (4,000 credits/mo, $4 per 100 additional credits)
cofounder.starter_prompt: Connect to my Gmail, Slack, and Google Calendar. Every morning at 8am, send me a message in Slack with: today's meetings and any prep context, emails that arrived overnight that need a response, and my top 3 unfinished tasks from yesterday.
getting_started_steps: Sign up for free at app.cofounder.co — 3,000 credits included, no credit card required
Connect your key tools: Gmail, Slack, Google Calendar — click 'Integrations' and authorize each one
In the chat, describe your first automation in plain English — Cofounder will build the workflow for you
Review what it built, test it with one run, and approve it to go live
Watch the 'Automations' tab to see it running — and keep adding new workflows by describing them in chat
suggested_prompts: Every morning at 8am, post a Slack briefing with today's meetings, overnight emails that need responses, and my top 3 carry-over tasks
When a new lead fills out our Typeform, research their company, score the lead, and add a summary to our HubSpot CRM
Every Friday at 4pm, pull updates from our project management tool and post a weekly status summary in #team-updates on Slack
Monitor our competitor's pricing page and alert me in Slack if anything changes

poke.name: Poke
poke.summary: A proactive AI assistant that lives in your existing messages — iMessage, SMS, and WhatsApp. Connects to your email and calendar, then texts you what you need before you ask: meeting briefs, email flags, follow-up reminders. No app to download. Built by the team behind Interaction Co, backed by General Catalyst at a $100M valuation.
poke.best_for: Busy executives who want a zero-friction AI assistant that works through text messages — no new app, no learning curve, just texts
poke.limitations: Still early-stage — some features (like drafting replies to emails) are still in development. Privacy trade-off: requires access to your inbox and calendar. Pricing not publicly listed — negotiated or waitlist-based for now. iOS-centric (iMessage works best on iPhone).
poke.cost: $5–50/mo (pricing confirmed on request via poke.com — not publicly listed)
poke.starter_prompt: Connect to my Google Calendar and Gmail. Every morning at 7:30am, text me a brief: today's meetings with prep context for each one, and any emails I haven't responded to in over 24 hours.
getting_started_steps: Visit poke.com (link above) and sign up — Poke will text you from a number you'll recognize
Follow the setup prompts to grant access to your Gmail and Google Calendar
You'll receive a text — just reply like you're messaging a friend to set preferences
Paste the starter prompt below in your first message to configure your morning brief
From now on, Poke texts you proactively — no app to open, no prompts to write
suggested_prompts: Every morning at 7:30am, text me my schedule for the day with 2-3 sentences of prep context for each meeting
Flag any emails I haven't replied to in over 24 hours and text me a summary each evening at 6pm
When I have a meeting with someone new, research them on LinkedIn and text me a brief 30 minutes before
Every Friday, text me a list of anyone I met with this week that I should follow up with

n8n_custom.name: Custom Agent (n8n + Claude API)
n8n_custom.summary: Build your own agent orchestration layer using n8n workflows wired to the Claude API. Full control over logic, memory, integrations, triggers, and data handling. The only option on this list that can be designed to exactly match a specific business process — with no compromise. This is a project, not a product.
n8n_custom.best_for: Companies with specific, high-value workflows that no off-the-shelf tool handles well — custom routing logic, proprietary data, compliance requirements, or complex multi-system orchestration
n8n_custom.limitations: This is not a plug-and-play tool — it requires discovery, design, and build time (weeks, not hours). n8n Cloud runs €20–€50/mo for most workflows; Claude API costs vary by usage. Requires an implementation partner unless you have an in-house technical team.
n8n_custom.cost: n8n Cloud €20–€50/mo + Claude API costs ($50–500+/mo depending on volume) + implementation time
n8n_custom.starter_prompt: This agent is custom-built for your specific workflow. The first step is a discovery call with Optemization to design the right system for your needs.
getting_started_steps: Visit optemization.com (link above) and book a discovery call
Describe your top 3 workflows that would have the most business impact if automated
Optemization will design the agent architecture — what it connects to, how it makes decisions, where it stores memory
Build and deploy: typically 2–6 weeks depending on complexity
Ongoing: the agent improves over time as it processes more of your business data
suggested_prompts: When a new lead comes in via our form, enrich it with company data, score it against our ICP, and route it to the right sales rep in Slack with a full brief
Every Monday morning, pull data from our CRM, support tool, and analytics into a single board report draft and email it to leadership
Monitor incoming support tickets for escalation patterns — alert the team lead in Slack when a customer has contacted us 3+ times in 7 days
Automatically review inbound contracts: extract payment terms, liability clauses, and IP ownership, then flag any non-standard terms for legal review

tasks.label: Chasing my team for status updates
Prepping for meetings and following up after

manus.complexity: Manus is already in your Meta Ads Manager — zero setup required.
manus.pitch: We help marketing teams build reporting workflows and audience research automations on top of Manus so nothing falls through the cracks.

claude_cowork.complexity: Download Claude Desktop, subscribe to Pro or Max, and you're running in under 10 minutes.
claude_cowork.pitch: We can define the workflows, build the templates, and configure the right folder structure so Cowork delivers consistent output from day one.

openclaw.complexity: Easiest path: sign up at coral.inc (7-day free trial, $50/mo). Self-hosting requires a VPS, Docker, and a free afternoon.
openclaw.pitch: We configure OpenClaw deployments end-to-end — integrations, automations, memory architecture, and the skills that make it actually useful.

notion_custom_agents.complexity: Available now on Notion Business. Enable in Settings → Agents and start building — no technical work needed.
notion_custom_agents.pitch: We've designed agent-ready Notion workspaces for dozens of clients. We know exactly where the edges are and how to build agents that hold up at scale.

claude_code.complexity: Install the CLI (one command), subscribe to Claude Pro or Max, and start coding. Developer setup takes 10 minutes.
claude_code.pitch: We use Claude Code daily to build agentic systems for clients. We can help your engineering team get the most out of it.

codex.complexity: Included in all paid ChatGPT plans — connect your GitHub repo at chatgpt.com/codex and assign your first task.
codex.pitch: We're model-agnostic. If your team is already in the OpenAI ecosystem, we'll build the workflows that make Codex actually reliable.

perplexity_computer.complexity: Sign up for Perplexity Max ($200/mo) — Computer access is included. Cloud-based, no install needed.
perplexity_computer.pitch: We help teams structure their research workflows so they get consistent, actionable intelligence — not just long documents.

cofounder.complexity: Sign up free at app.cofounder.co, connect your tools, and describe your first automation. Running in under 15 minutes.
cofounder.pitch: We can design the right automation architecture and make sure your Cofounder setup scales cleanly as your team and tools grow.

poke.complexity: Sign up at poke.com, grant calendar and email access — Poke texts you from there. No app to install.
poke.pitch: We help executives figure out which AI tools actually get adopted and make sure Poke is configured to match how you actually work.

n8n_custom.complexity: This is a project, not a product — expect a 2–6 week build. Plan accordingly.
n8n_custom.pitch: This is exactly what we build. We design the architecture so your custom agent compounds in value over time, not just automates one thing.
